description: Value bonds arbitrage-free — law of one price, binomial interest rate trees, calibration to term structure, pathwise valuation, Monte Carlo, equilibrium and arbitrage-free term structure models

meaning_of_arbitrage_free.law_of_one_price: Identical cash flows must have identical prices
meaning_of_arbitrage_free.arbitrage_opportunity: Riskless profit with zero net investment
meaning_of_arbitrage_free.implications_fi: Bonds with same cash flows in all states must have same price

arbitrage_free_option_free.method: Discount each cash flow at maturity-matching spot rate

binomial_interest_rate_tree.structure: Lognormal tree of one-period rates with up/down nodes
binomial_interest_rate_tree.volatility_input: σ governs spread between adjacent nodes (e.g., u/d = e^{2σ})

determining_node_value.discount_at_node_rate: Use one-period rate at that node

calibration.objective: Tree must price benchmark bonds (zero curve) exactly
calibration.process: Iteratively solve for short rates so model prices match observed

valuing_option_free.binomial: Backward induction on the calibrated tree

pathwise_valuation.method: Average present value across all 2^n paths

monte_carlo.process: Simulate many rate paths; discount along each; average
monte_carlo.benchmark: Calibrate so expected value matches benchmark prices

term_structure_models.equilibrium_models: CIR (cir > 0 mean reverting), Vasicek (Gaussian, mean reverting); derive curve from primitives
term_structure_models.arbitrage_free_models: Ho-Lee, BDT, HJM; fit observed curve exactly
term_structure_models.modern_models: Affine multi-factor; LMM for swaption pricing

errors.message: valuation_method must be one of the supported methods

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use equilibrium models without checking fit to observed curve
anti_patterns.why: CIR and Vasicek may misprice the live curve by 50-100 bps; for trading, arbitrage-free models (Ho-Lee, BDT, HJM) are required to ensure no static arbitrage

anti_patterns.rule: Do not value path-dependent securities with backward induction alone
anti_patterns.why: MBS prepayment depends on rate path, not just current rate; backward induction averages incorrectly — Monte Carlo is required

anti_patterns.rule: Do not skip calibration of binomial trees to benchmark prices
anti_patterns.why: An uncalibrated tree produces theoretical values inconsistent with observable bond prices, exposing the user to immediate model arbitrage losses

goals.description: Value bonds arbitrage-free — law of one price, binomial interest rate trees, calibration to term structure, pathwise valuation, Monte Carlo, equilibrium and arbitrage-free term structure models

success_metrics.measurement: All decisions have complete audit trails

constraints.description: All operations must be auditable and traceable

tradeoffs.reason: trading operations require precise execution and full audit trails

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
